attestations.verbatim_span: The biological and geographic data are much more suited to reliable analysis.

claims.text: The author's arguments will be accessible and transparent, in contrast to opaque complex modeling used in some well-known papers.

attestations.verbatim_span: Although parts may look abstract to non-mathematical readers, all the arguments will be accessible and transparent, in contrast to the opaque complex modeling used in some well-known papers.
attestations.framing: contrasts own transparency against "opaque complex modeling" of unnamed other papers

claims.text: Demaneuf and De Maistre's 2020 Bayesian analysis concluded that P(ZW) and P(ZL) were about equal.

attestations.verbatim_span: Demaneuf and  De Maistre wrote up a Bayesian discussion of that issue in 2020, concluding that the probability of ZW, i.e. P(ZW), and  the probability of ZL, i.e. P(ZL), were about equal.

claims.text: Much of Demaneuf and De Maistre's analysis of prior probabilities is similar to the arguments used in this piece.

attestations.verbatim_span: Much of their analysis, particularly of prior probabilities, is close to the arguments I use here, but written more gracefully and with more thorough documentation.

claims.text: Demaneuf and De Maistre's method of accounting for uncertainty, while different from the author's, is transparent and rational, unlike some other estimates.

attestations.verbatim_span: They use a different way of accounting for uncertainties than I do, but unlike some other estimates their method is transparent and rational.
attestations.framing: contrasts favorably against unnamed "other estimates"

claims.text: The analysis will compare P(ZW) to P(LL) rather than P(ZL), because sequence data indicate lab involvement in generating the viral sequence, meaning P(ZL) is smaller than P(LL).

attestations.verbatim_span: here I’ll just compare the probability P(ZW) to that of the LL lab account, P(LL), because sequence data point to a lab involvement in generating the viral sequence, so that P(ZL) will itself be smaller than P(LL).

claims.text: Sequence data point to lab involvement in generating the viral sequence.

attestations.verbatim_span: sequence data point to a lab involvement in generating the viral sequence

claims.text: The prior odds heavily favor ZW as the historically common origin of most new epidemics.

attestations.verbatim_span: I’ll start with odds that heavily favor ZW, historically the common origin of most new epidemics.

claims.text: The location of the initial outbreak in Wuhan, where a major research lab had submitted a grant proposal to modify bat coronaviruses similarly to features later found in SC2 and had noted lab safety difficulties, is an important update factor.

attestations.verbatim_span: The most immediately obvious is that the location of the initial outbreak was Wuhan, the location of a major research lab that had submitted a grant proposal that included modifying bat coronaviruses in the way later found in SC2 and that had already been noted for difficulties with lab safety.

claims.text: The Wuhan location coincidence could occur by chance under ZW, but can be approximately converted to a factor objectively increasing the odds of LL.

attestations.verbatim_span: That location could also have occurred by accidental coincidence for ZW, but we shall see that it’s not hard to approximately convert the coincidence to a factor objectively increasing the odds of LL.

claims.text: The consensus prior view is that P(LL) is much less than P(ZW), treating ZW as the null hypothesis/boring first guess.

attestations.verbatim_span: I’ll start with a consensus view, that the prior guess would be that P(LL) is much less than P(ZW). That corresponds to the standard idea that you would call ZW the null hypothesis, i.e. the boring first guess.

claims.text: The author treats ZW as initially quantitatively more probable by a crudely estimated factor rather than as a qualitatively sacred null hypothesis.

attestations.verbatim_span: Rather than treat the null as qualitatively sacred I’ll just leave it as initially quantitatively more probable by a crudely estimated factor.

claims.text: Both P(ZW) and P(LL) derive from sums of tiny probabilities across individuals.

attestations.verbatim_span: Both P(ZW) and P(LL) come from sums of tiny probabilities for each individual person.

claims.text: P(LL) comes mostly from a sum over individuals in Wuhan.

attestations.verbatim_span: P(LL) comes mostly from a sum over individuals in Wuhan.

claims.text: P(ZW) comes from a sum over a much larger set of individuals across China and southeast Asia.

attestations.verbatim_span: P(ZW) comes from a sum over a much larger set of individuals spread over China and southeast Asia.

claims.text: Since the pandemic is known to have started in Wuhan, restricting probability sums to the Wuhan area barely changes P(LL) but eliminates most contributions to P(ZW).

attestations.verbatim_span: Since we know with confidence that this pandemic started in Wuhan, restricting the sum of individual probabilities to people around Wuhan doesn’t change P(LL) much but eliminates most of the contributions to P(ZW).

claims.text: The Wuhan population fact implies the P(LL)/P(ZW) odds should be increased by about a factor of 100.

attestations.verbatim_span: That means we need to increase whatever P(LL)/P(ZW) odds we started with by about a factor of 100, since the denominator is reduced.

probability_estimates.value: factor of 100 increase in P(LL)/P(ZW) odds

claims.text: The furin cleavage site (FCS) genetic sequence update is the most important single update to the odds calculation.

attestations.verbatim_span: The most important single update will come from a special genetic sequence that codes for the furin cleavage site (FCS) where the UNC-WIV-EHA DEFUSE  proposal suggested adding a tiny piece of protein sequence to a natural coronavirus sequence.

claims.text: The DEFUSE proposal suggested adding a small protein sequence piece to a natural coronavirus sequence, similar to the FCS found in SC2.

attestations.verbatim_span: the UNC-WIV-EHA DEFUSE  proposal suggested adding a tiny piece of protein sequence to a natural coronavirus sequence

claims.text: The FCS's nucleotide coding is almost never found elsewhere in SC2 or its relatives but is typical of known designed sequences such as mRNA vaccines.

attestations.verbatim_span: The tiny extra part of SC2’s spike protein, the FCS that is absent in its wild relatives, has nucleotide coding that is almost never found in other parts of SC2 or in its relatives but is typical for the most relevant known designed sequences– e.g. the mRNA vaccines.

claims.text: On a map of RNA sequences, SC2 appears in a location almost uninhabited naturally but populated by lab sequences.

attestations.verbatim_span: One can picture this via a map representing RNA sequences, in which SC2 appeared at a location that is almost uninhabited naturally but where a bustling city of lab sequences resides.

claims.text: The FCS sequence coincidence is even more numerically striking than the location coincidence.

attestations.verbatim_span: This coincidence turns out to be even more numerically striking than for the physical location.

claims.text: After incorporating the location and FCS updates, P(LL) becomes much larger than P(ZW), even starting from a generous prior favoring ZW.

attestations.verbatim_span: Once these two simple updates are included we’ll have P(LL) much larger than P(ZW) even if we start with a generously high but plausible preference for ZW.

claims.text: Additional updates, including some claimed to favor ZW, will further swing the odds toward LL.

attestations.verbatim_span: A look at other updates, both ones claimed to favor ZW and some of the ones claimed to favor LL,  will swing the odds further toward LL.

claims.text: P(ZW) will shrink to about 1%, limited from shrinking further only by allowance for uncertainties.

attestations.verbatim_span: P(ZW) will shrink to about 1%, and is saved from shrinking much further only by  allowance for uncertainties.

claims.text: This crude, approximate style of calculation is comparable to Fermi-style calculations routinely used by physicists.

attestations.verbatim_span: This openly crude and approximate form of argument may alarm readers who are not accustomed to the Fermi-style calculations routinely used by physicists.

claims.text: In Fermi-style calculations, minor distinctions between similar factors are not worried about because the arguments are not that precise.

attestations.verbatim_span: In this sort of calculation one doesn’t worry much about minor distinctions between similar factors, e.g. 8 and 12, because the arguments are not generally that precise.

claims.text: Although large uncertainties sometimes render such calculations useless, this is not one of those cases.

attestations.verbatim_span: Sometimes the large uncertainties in such a calculation render the conclusion useless, but this turns out not to be one of those cases.

claims.text: Bayes' Theorem provides the rule for updating prior odds with new evidence to obtain posterior odds

attestations.verbatim_span: Bayes’ Theorem provides the rule for how to do this.

claims.text: Posterior odds equal prior odds times the product of likelihood ratios for independent pieces of evidence

attestations.verbatim_span: P(LL)/P(ZW)  = (P­ 0 (LL)/P­ 0 (ZW) )*(P(obs 1 |LL)/P(obs 1 |ZW))*(P(obs 2 |LL)/P(obs 2 |ZW))…… *(P(obs n |LL)/P(obs n |ZW))
attestations.context: Explanation of Bayesian updating with multiple observations

claims.text: Both prior odds and likelihood ratios involve subjective estimates requiring a range of plausible values, i.e. robust Bayesian methods

attestations.verbatim_span: not only the prior odds P­ 0 (LL)/P­ 0 (ZW) but also the likelihoods involve some subjective estimates. In order to obtain a convincing answer we need to include some range of plausible values for each likelihood ratio, i.e. use robust Bayesian methods

claims.text: Including uncertainties in the analysis tends to pull the final odds back from an extreme value toward one

attestations.verbatim_span: realistic recognition of the uncertainties will tend to pull the final odds back from an extreme value towards one

claims.text: Taking logarithms of the odds factors converts the product into a sum, since expected value of a sum equals sum of expected values

attestations.verbatim_span: Since the expected value of a sum is just the sum of the expected values it’s convenient to convert the product to a sum by taking the logarithms of all the factors.

claims.text: The final odds estimate is derived from a logit distribution centered on the sum of logits with width from the square root of sum of squares of standard errors, assuming independence of errors

attestations.verbatim_span: The final odds estimate will be obtained from a logit distribution centered on the sum of the logits with a width determined by the square root of the sum of the squares of the standard errors, since the errors in different factors are presumed to be independent of each other.

claims.text: The analysis approximates the net logit distribution as Gaussian to calculate net odds accounting for uncertainty

attestations.verbatim_span: A further approximation, treating the net logit distribution as Gaussian, will then allow us to calculate net odds taking the uncertainties of the factors into account.

claims.text: The author will not omit features that favor ZW but will drop some features favoring LL

attestations.verbatim_span: I will not omit any that I think would favor ZW but will drop some that I think tend to favor LL.
attestations.context: Methodological disclosure of selection bias mitigation

claims.text: Peculiar features of the virus under ZW will not be penalized if those features could have a selective advantage, since we only observe the virus after evolution that could amplify peculiar accidents

attestations.verbatim_span: The peculiarity of some features under ZW will not be used to penalize ZW’s odds if those features are likely to have a notable selective advantage,  since we are only viewing the virus after an evolution that can selectively amplify peculiar accidents.

claims.text: Small factors with unambiguous sign will be included, such as the lack of detection of a wildlife host

attestations.verbatim_span: I will include some small factors when the sign of their logit is unambiguous, e.g. a factor from the lack of any detection of a wildlife host.

claims.text: If there were more than one spillover, either all or none were lab-related, a point of unanimity used to simplify the analysis

attestations.verbatim_span: In combining the lineage and case location data we can simplify a bit by using one point on which there is unanimity– if there were more than one spillover either all or none were lab-related.

claims.text: Worobey et al. used case location data to argue HSM was the location of spillover to humans, not just a superspreading site

claims.text: Worobey et al. argued it would require a remarkable coincidence, with probability ~1/400, for HSM to be the first ascertained spreading site unless it were the actual spillover site

attestations.verbatim_span: Worobey et al. argue that since there were hundreds of plausible superspreading locations it would require a remarkable coincidence, with probability ~1/400, for a possible spillover site, HSM, to be the first ascertained spreading site unless it were the actual spillover site.

claims.text: The first notable ascertained Beijing outbreak occurred at the Xinfadi wet market 56 days after initial cases were controlled, though this market could not have been the spillover site

attestations.verbatim_span: One can get a preliminary empirical feel for how much of a coincidence that would be by looking at the first notable ascertained outbreak in Beijing some 56 days after initial cases were controlled. It occurred at the Xinfadi wet market ,  which could not have been the site of the months-earlier spillover.
attestations.context: Empirical counter-example to Worobey et al.'s coincidence argument

On Saturday, the department reported 548 cases, Thailand’s biggest daily spike, sending shockwaves through a country that has seen only a small number of infections over the past several months due to strict border and quarantine controls.
attestations.context: Method 2, quoted news source on Thailand outbreak

claims.text: The Thailand outbreak was traced to a 67-year-old shrimp vendor at the seafood market

claims.text: In Singapore, a cluster of nearly 1200 cases was linked to a fishery port and wet markets, occurring months after zero Covid

attestations.verbatim_span: In Singapore, a cluster of nearly 1200 cases was linked to a fishery port (where seafood was sold to the public) and wet markets . This too happened months after zero Covid .

attestations.verbatim_span: Early clusters are exceptionally rare because zero Covid periods were infrequent worldwide, and typically, each period would yield only one early cluster.

claims.text: The occurrence of two such market-linked cases in Thailand and Singapore, and 2 out of 5 major clusters during China's 2020 zero Covid period, underscores their significance

attestations.verbatim_span: The occurrence of two such cases in Thailand and Singapore, as well as 2 out of 5 major clusters during China’s 2020 zero Covid period, underscores their significance.

claims.text: Cold wet surfaces abundant in seafood markets most likely provide a major advantage compared to other crowded locations

attestations.verbatim_span: Most likely, the cold wet surfaces abundant in seafood markets provide a major advantage compared to other crowded locations.

attestations.verbatim_span: Such a denominator is not accurately known, as early clusters are not always identified or documented.

claims.text: An upper limit of 100 is placed on the denominator, giving a ratio of over 4%

attestations.verbatim_span: Given that not many countries achieved zero Covid, and often the early cluster is not easy to find, we place an upper limit of 100, for a ratio of over 4%.

claims.text: There is enough leeway to confidently claim the conditional probability is above 1%, allowing for differences between HSM and these markets

attestations.verbatim_span: There could be some differences between HSM and these markets (one possibility discussed below), leaving us enough leeway to confidently claim the conditional probability is above 1%.

claims.text: Those impressed by the coincidence of the pandemic starting in WIV's city and then in a wildlife market should be even more impressed that a shrimp vendor is again the index case in an early cluster

attestations.verbatim_span: Those who, like Scott, were impressed by the coincidence that the pandemic started in the city of WIV, and then were more impressed by the coincidence that it started in a market hosting wildlife, should now be even more impressed by the coincidence that a shrimp vendor is again the index case in an early cluster.

attestations.framing: sarcastic framing targeting Scott Alexander's reasoning as inconsistent

claims.text: Rules-of-thumb thinking about coincidences is bound to result in wrong conclusions, and there is no alternative to rigorous probabilistic analysis avoiding human bias

attestations.verbatim_span: This kind of rules-of-thumb thinking is bound to result in wrong conclusions. There is sadly no alternative to a proper rigorous probabilistic analysis of all evidence using a methodology that avoids human bias.

claims.text: The CDC-move hypothesis was not viewed as the best explanation by the author, but becomes the best one for those rejecting other explanations

claims.text: This makes the supposed 1/10,000 coincidence a 1/100, supporting the 1% conditional probability estimate

attestations.verbatim_span: This makes the supposed 1/10,000 coincidence a 1/100, which again supports the 1% conditional probability estimate.

claims.text: The debate structuring failure is fully Rootclaim's responsibility, not the judges' fault

claims.text: Rootclaim found many mistakes throughout both judges' decisions but will focus on three most important ones

claims.text: It is a mistake to consider HSM a random location in Wuhan that would form an early cluster only once every 10,000 hypothetical SARS2 lab leaks

attestations.verbatim_span: it’s obvious that it is a mistake to consider HSM to be a random location in Wuhan (i.e. will form an early cluster only once every 10,000 hypothetical SARS2 lab leaks in Wuhan)

claims.text: Even without a perfect model for why HSM is a likely early cluster location, the complexity of virus spread in urban areas means there's no way to reach confidence for a number as extreme as p=0.0001

attestations.verbatim_span: Even though we were not able to provide a perfect model for why HSM is a likely early cluster location, the complexity of a virus spreading in an urban area, and especially the huge difference that a small exponential advantage at HSM will have on the final numbers, means there is no way to reach anywhere close to the level of confidence required to claim a number as extreme as p=0.0001.

claims.text: The debate shows significant disagreement on HSM's role as evidence, questioning how much this coincidence favors zoonosis

claims.text: The judges reached prior likelihood numbers for lab leak below 0.01
